title: Fix fork inline
body:
If a forked process contains no awaits, it can be inlined. However, currently it's happening in the wrong place – while traversing the AST in `V3Timing`. The problem with this approach is that awaits can be created after we visit a fork. For example:
```systemverilog
class Cls;
   task dly;
       #1;
   endtask
endclass
…
Cls obj = new;
fork
   obj.dly;  // This process could get inlined
join_none
```
If the fork is visited before the `dly` task, the `obj.dly;` part would get inlined, as it would have no await at this point. Only later, after `dly` is visited, the `obj.dly;` call would be wrapped in a `co_await`, resulting in the delay being part of the main process.

This PR moves the inlining to a later stage when all awaits are already in place.

Preferably, this should be merged after #3593. Pre-PR to #3595.
